executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations victimized in a single 48-hour period. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart with a confirmed database leak, and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group alone claimed 20 Canadian victims spanning healthcare, education, manufacturing, and critical infrastructure. Multiple threat groups are actively coordinating or simultaneously exploiting Canadian targets across all sectors, suggesting either a coordinated campaign or opportunistic feeding frenzy on compromised environments.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. High-impact victims include healthcare facilities (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Labtician Ophthalmics), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, Teccart College), critical infrastructure (Eeyou Communications Network), and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth). National Money Mart suffered a confirmed database breach by the Everest group. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) was compromised by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware, Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi, multiple accounting firms compromised (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant by Qilin)

report_2w.executive_summary: Canadian organizations must immediately patch critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) flagged by CCCS in alert AL25-012. A 13-year-old Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability (CVE-2026-34197) is now under active exploitation, prompting a federal patch mandate from CISA. The supply chain remains under heavy attack: Checkmarx's official KICS Docker repository was trojanized by TeamPCP actors, Bitwarden CLI was compromised to spread credential stealers, and malicious npm packages tied to Namastex.ai are actively deploying CanisterWorm malware. Major breaches this period include UK Biobank (500,000 medical records sold on Alibaba), French government ANTS database (19 million identity records), Rituals cosmetics (millions of customer records), and Vercel (customer data theft). The Lazarus group continues developer-targeted attacks via backdoored coding tests, while Sinobi ransomware has claimed 277 victims (79% in the US). Anthropic's Mythos AI tool found 271 Firefox vulnerabilities, but reports indicate unauthorized groups may have gained access to this offensive capability.

items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File) - backdoored coding tests to compromise developer systems

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning of critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) requiring immediate patching by Canadian organizations. Checkmarx supply chain compromise affects Canadian development teams using KICS Docker images. No specific incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
